source.title: Calypso Casino defensive setup guide (all sites)
source.notes: Auto-generated captions with a fair amount of ASR noise on room names (e.g. 'brister' for Barista, 'boker'/'bo-' for Poker, creator repeatedly mis-says 'green' for Managers/Manager's Office and self-corrects). Creator explicitly states this is a personal/evolving setup, not a definitive meta, and says he may revise it in future videos. He rates the four sites, worst to best: Top Floor (thinks it's genuinely weak and wouldn't defend it by choice), then Blackjack/Poker and Bar & Betting (roughly interchangeable, leaning Bar & Betting), then Basement (his favourite). No operator names beyond a small set are used (Kaid, Smoke, Maestro, Jager) — many recommendations are role/gadget-based ('a DMR', 'a shield') rather than tied to a named operator, and are recorded that way rather than guessing a specific operator's name. Rewrite pass: could not obtain reliable compass-orientation grounding for this map (official Ubisoft blueprint PNGs contain no room labels or north indicator, and no third-party callout map with confirmed orientation exists yet for this newly-released map) — locations below are left as room/feature names only, without compass prefixes, per spec rule 8.

reinforcements.note: Right-hand side of the Barista wall / left-hand side of the Pool wall.
Third reinforcement in the Kitchen area.
Reinforce both.
Reinforce both. Kaid-trick if you have Kaid and two ADS available.

headHoles.note: Head hole here to fire onto the breach or the mini bar depending which side attackers come through.

rotates.note: Keep this rotate small, on the left-hand side.
Rotate on the left side from Cigar / right side from Kitchen. Keep it tight to the right-hand side.
Strong for Smoke — smoke the bar plant or the breach from this one spot.
Paired with the head hole on the breach side of the same wall.

gadgetPlacements.note: Feet holes to see attackers before they see you.
Trap the pipe. Only challengeable from one angle.
Add a rotate here so an attacker holding this window from outside doesn't fully cut off Fish Stairs.
Shoot both out to remove audio giveaways. Stand on the jukebox for an angle over the soft wall into Lobby.
Watch Tequila or the breach push — either leaves the other angle exposed.
Shield placement here covers both sides; turn to cover whichever side attackers push.

operatorRecommendations.note: Kaid-trick the Kitchen wall with two ADS if available.
Smoke the bar plant or the breach from one position.
Traps the pipe near the common Coat Check entry point.

generalTips.note: Weakest site on the map per the creator — easiest to learn but favors attackers.
Barista is critical to hold but hard to hold long-term — no vault-up angle over the counter, two common attacker entry points, and once the Kitchen breach is open there's only one way out.
Watch for a DMR/ACOG popping out low at the Barista window and holding the door across the way.
Lobby itself is effectively unholdable (skylight repels, multiple windows/doors). Expect Barista/Pool to get flushed within about a minute under a Ying/Grim/Blitz or Blackbeard push.

reinforcements.note: Core reinforcement.
Core reinforcement.
Core reinforcement.
Strong plant spot — reinforce and tuck behind the counter to watch it and the nearby rotate. Main follow-up angle to worry about is Yellow Door, typically hard-breached.

reinforcements.location: Cash/Green Wall Into Managers, Right-Hand Side
reinforcements.note: Ninth recommended reinforcement alongside Vault, Yellow, and Green.
Reinforce this instead of the elevator hatch or an extra wall.

headHoles.note: Hold into Vault and see/smoke over the top. Also covers the Yellow wall from this angle.
Small rat angle catching anyone coming around the side of Vault via the stacked boxes.
Watch Walk-in from Yellow or Weapon Cache. Paired with a punch hole below — attackers see the head hole first, then get caught low.
Shotgun hole to hold through the elevator door as well as the main angle.

rotates.note: Needed so attackers can't reach Managers and then Cash unopposed within about 40 seconds.
Connects CCTV and the Vault Checkpoint room.

hatches.note: Leave soft/not reinforced — the drop doesn't give attackers much and Yellow Door is an easy entry anyway.
Leave open. Start upstairs, watch the Manager's breach and Green Stairs, get early drone reads/delay, pre-place C4 (e.g. Valkyrie) before dropping back down if the wall below isn't open.

gadgetPlacements.note: Ceiling above is destructible — protect wall-denial gadgets from grenades lobbed over the top with one or two ADS.
Same nade-over-the-top risk as the Vault wall, less pronounced here.
Shield placement here, holding through the head holes from behind it.
Pre-place C4 near the stairwell opening as long as the nearby breach isn't open.

operatorRecommendations.note: Protects wall-denial gadgets on the Vault and Yellow walls from nade-over-the-top plays through the destructible ceiling.
Watches Manager's breach and Green Stairs from above, pre-places C4 on the stairwell opening, rotates down through the Green hatch if needed.
Holds the strongest single room in the basement — covers Vault, the Yellow wall angle, and the Vault-boxes rat angle.

generalTips.note: Creator's favourite site on the map, but easily lost if not set up and defended correctly.
Site is vast with many entry points — a lone defender near a 3-4 person push through Vault and Yellow simultaneously will get overwhelmed quickly.
Don't give up Cash too soon — if attackers open the Manager's wall and reach Cash within about a minute, hold out there.
Most of the CCTV-side ceiling is destructible; none of the Vault ceiling is. Watch for attackers making a vert angle behind the bar upstairs.
Use the elevation-change spots on the Yellow side — most players aim at head height by default, so being higher creates a harder fight.

reinforcements.note: Core reinforcements for the site.
Also playable from the Secret-right side.

reinforcements.location: Blackjack Wall Into Bathroom, Left Side
reinforcements.note: Also playable from Bathroom's right side.
Reinforce both.
Optional.

reinforcements.location: Two Upstairs Humidor Walls Into Kitchen
reinforcements.note: Reinforce both so the upstairs Humidor room can be held.

headHoles.note: Go prone here for a tighter angle in addition to the standing angle.
Pair a head hole with a punch hole below it — attackers see the head hole first, get caught through the lower punch hole.

rotates.note: Play onto the breach and onto the Poker door from here.

rotates.location: Blackjack Wall Into Poker, Right Side
rotates.note: Standard rotate between Blackjack and Poker.
Holds the rotate from Secret, the common plant spot behind the bar, the door between sites, and the breach — all from one position.

hatches.note: Do not reinforce — it's the drop point back to site if the upstairs defender gets flushed.

gadgetPlacements.note: Barbed wire under this window for a near-guaranteed kill on anyone extending in.
Watch for a torch-hole cut across the top for a wide angle across the site — if you see one, don't peek it.
Common plant spot; watch it directly from the upstairs Humidor room.
Bring extra ADS in case attackers nade it. Hard to escape from once the breach is open and you're flushed.
DMR holds the long angle into the Poker site door.

operatorRecommendations.note: Bring extra ADS to counter nade pushes into Secret.
Holds the long angle toward the Poker door and the upstairs breach room.

generalTips.note: Creator is on the fence between this site and Bar & Betting for second-best, but rates both above Top Floor.
Playing upstairs (Humidor room) covers the rotate, plant spot, door between sites, and breach all at once.
Lobby is a nightmare for attackers to push through due to the number of angles.
If attackers control the upstairs Humidor room after opening the hatch, the round is usually lost barring a big play.
If the hatch can't be dropped to rotate back, come down Fish Stairs into Vending as a fast alternative.

reinforcements.note: First of seven reinforcements used on this site.
Fourth reinforcement.
Fifth and sixth reinforcements.
Seventh reinforcement, above the site in the Barista area.
Use if committing to holding Barista upstairs.

headHoles.note: Catch anyone coming down the bottom of the stairs — hard angle for attackers to spot.
Alternative to reinforcing this wall — holds the Janitor stairs instead.

rotates.note: Holds the side door, a tight angle on the hatch, and the wall attackers commonly breach here. Play behind the specific cover here or you'll get smoked from the hatch.
Leads to the top of Locker Stairs and the breach toward Janitor; peek out aggressively if that breach opens.
Get aggressive back into Janitor if needed; watch for attackers coming up Locker Stairs via the Lockers breach.

hatches.note: Hold a tight angle on it. Watch for getting smoked through it from either the bar side or the counter side.
Reinforce only if committing to Barista — doing so removes the quick escape route. Alternative: leave it soft, soak 3-4 drones' worth of utility upstairs, then drop once flushed.

gadgetPlacements.note: One camera here, one there, one between the sites to see both approach directions.
One of the safer spots to sit on cameras.
Watch this wall closely — a hard breach here gives early map control and a push up the Green Stairs.
Barbed wire, a proximity mine, or a Banshee-style trap for early warning.

operatorRecommendations.note: Runs a three-camera setup covering both approaches into the site plus the area between sites.

generalTips.note: Sledge or Buck (Attack-side) are likely to open the Manager's Office wall below this site for early vertical map control — watch that angle.
Creator slightly prefers this site to Blackjack/Poker, and prefers it to Top Floor.
Multiple spots on this site have gaps above walls that let attackers nade over wall denial — factor this into where you play.
The Bar/Betting counter rotate is the standout strong point of the site.
Side windows near the tucked-away Maestro spot are rarely repelled by attackers.
Playing upstairs in Barista to delay is worth it even expecting to die — buys a minute to a minute and a half.
Less flexible than Blackjack/Poker for late-round extends — if the Barista hatch is open there are a lot of cutoff angles.
